steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Determine what scale factor k must satisfy
steps.content: Side of T = k × side of S. For T's side to be **greater** than S's side, we need k > 1.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice numerically
steps.content:
$\frac{29}{28}$ $\frac{29}{28} \approx 1.036 \gt 1$ — T's sides are longer than S's ✓
B) $1$ — sides are equal, not greater
C) $\frac{28}{29} \approx 0.966 \lt 1$ — T's sides would be shorter
D) $0$ — sides collapse to length zero

steps.title: Select the valid value
steps.content: Only $\frac{29}{28}$ exceeds 1. That matches the correct answer — A is correct.

choiceAnalysis:
• '$\frac{29}{28}$': $\frac{29}{28} \approx 1.036 \gt 1$, so each side of T is strictly longer than the corresponding side of S. ✓
  • '$1$': k = 1 produces a congruent triangle — sides are equal, not greater. ✗
  • '$\frac{28}{29}$': $\frac{28}{29} \approx 0.966 \lt 1$, so each side of T would be shorter than S's sides. ✗
  • '$0$': k = 0 collapses every side to length 0 — not a valid triangle. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
